actionable_feedback: Resolve grader/verifier metadata inconsistency: choose a single canonical semantic score and passed boolean and update top-level fields to match final_score and embedded raw outputs (current semantic judgment = 0.6 < pass threshold 0.7 -> passed=false) OR provide explicit justification if the canonical decision is different.

In ci_policy.md, add explicit justification for each reconstructed weight (severity/recency/exposure): cite the Source_Packet fields or show the reconstruction math and why equal weights were chosen; include an alternatives/sensitivity table showing how different plausible weight choices change threshold crossings and budgets.

In build_cache_roi.xlsx, add a dedicated 'Assumptions' sheet with: (a) one row per reconstructed item (weights, metric definitions, cache-hit treatment) that links to the Source_Packet cell (via text citation) and shows the exact formula or cell references used in scoring; (b) embedded cell-level comments or text provenance cells so a reviewer can inspect formula lineage without opening separate docs.

Clear or justify any Checks! cells that currently read NEEDS_REVIEW (e.g., Checks!G12). Either resolve the underlying ambiguity in the model or provide explicit rationale in the Checks sheet referencing the Assumptions and Source_Packet.

Re-run artifact validation in an environment that can parse xlsx (openpyxl available) and attach updated workbook validation metadata (formula_count, key_cached_values) so the grader/verifier can perform cell-level checks. Ensure agent_trace.json and actual_outputs.json are present and follow the canonical trace schema requested in the task trace error message.
Add tie-breaking rules and boundary-case handling to ci_policy.md (for scores exactly at threshold, for equal cost-benefit choices, handling of null/missing fields).

Expose summary check-cells (Summary sheet) that report: selected_combination, selected_cost_usd, selected_modeled_daily_benefit_usd, payback days, and a single pass/fail flag for 'policy_ready' (OK / OK_WITH_NEEDS_REVIEW / NEEDS_REVIEW) that exactly maps to the textual policy.

After making changes, produce a short reviewer note (1 paragraph) summarizing which previously 'needs_review' items were resolved and pointing to the exact workbook cells and policy paragraphs that changed.

feedback_summary: Deliverables present and largely correct, but semantic gaps and metadata inconsistencies prevent full credit. Key problems: (1) reconstructed scoring weights and some metric-to-driver mappings are documented as 'needs_review' without sufficient justification or cell-level provenance, (2) the xlsx preview could not be parsed in this environment (openpyxl_unavailable) so direct formula provenance could not be inspected, and (3) the grader output contains an internal inconsistency where top-level score/passed flags contradict the model's semantic final_score and pass/fail rationale. Resolve these to reach full credit.

recommended_next_steps: Fix grader metadata inconsistency so 'score' and 'passed' reflect the semantic judgement or document override.
Embed cell-level provenance and Assumptions sheet in workbook.
Re-run validation with openpyxl and include resulting validation metadata.
Resubmit and attach a 1-paragraph reviewer note summarizing resolved items.
